$description: Machine-readable frontend/backend parity contract ledger for Passage Zero. Enforced by scripts/check-frontend-backend-parity.js (pnpm test:parity). Do not hand-edit status to 'implemented' or set frontend.user_visible=true without a real, existing route/component -- the checker verifies file existence, not intent.
owner_scope_note: PM defines each contract before implementation; Engineering advances UI, durable state, RLS, commands, events, and recovery in the same slice; independent QA rejects drift before status promotion.

statuses.implemented: A reachable frontend route/component AND a real backend server command/durable table/RLS predicate/failure states are both wired together and evidenced.
statuses.backend_only: The backend server command, durable tables, RLS predicate, and (if applicable) append-only event exist and are evidenced, but no frontend route or component currently calls it. frontend.user_visible must be false.
statuses.queued: Neither side is built yet, or only partial groundwork exists. Only the planned target is documented. Must not claim a reachable UI (frontend.user_visible must be false, route/component must be null).

contracts.capability: Director/owner creates a staff invitation scoped to an organization and one or more locations

frontend.note: The form is reachable only for an authenticated owner/director on the exact isolated greenfield Preview route. Both the route boundary and Server Action re-check isolated-project configuration, active organization membership, role, and location authority before invoking the command.

contracts.action_status: ready -> submitting -> pending_acceptance receipt. A first command returns a one-time raw invitation token plus the durable invitation id and persisted created_at; same-key replay returns the same id/timestamp with no raw token or duplicate rows/events.
contracts.server_command: public.create_employee_invitation_idempotent_v2(p_organization_id uuid, p_invited_email text, p_organization_location_ids uuid[], p_purpose text, p_expires_at timestamptz, p_creation_request_id uuid) -> passage_private.create_employee_invitation_idempotent_v2

durable_tables.cardinality: one row per invitation; unique token_digest; terminal-state check prevents accepted+revoked simultaneously
one row per (invitation, location); composite primary key

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: passage_private.can_manage_invitation(id) -> passage_private.can_manage_organization (active membership, role in owner/director) AND passage_private.can_manage_location for every location on the invitation (active grant to an owner/director member)
failure_recovery_states: route-disabled outside the exact isolated Preview runtime, on the wrong project ref, or when password Preview auth is disabled
same-user retry uses the same idempotency key and returns the original durable id/created_at without exposing the one-time token again
already-active-member (Postgres 23505: 'This person is already an active organization member')
missing active organization membership / actor authority (Postgres 42501)
environment-unavailable (Supabase client cannot be constructed, e.g. missing env vars)
contracts.persona_projection: Visible only to the inviting owner/director's organization; invited_email and purpose are never exposed to family, staff, or vendor personas. The select policy restricts rows to invited_by_user_id, accepted_by_user_id, or passage_private.can_manage_invitation(id).

contracts.capability: Invited staff member inspects an invitation (inviter, organization, role, locations, purpose, expiry, state) before signing in

frontend.note: Server component renders the inspection result via public.inspect_organization_invitation before any sign-in is required, and fails closed on invalid/malformed tokens (validInvitationToken).

contracts.action_status: invitation_state resolves to one of available | accepted | revoked | expired on every page load

durable_tables.cardinality: one row per invitation, looked up by sha256 token_digest

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: public.inspect_organization_invitation is SECURITY INVOKER over a SECURITY DEFINER passage_private core, granted to anon+authenticated; the private schema itself is not exposed by the Data API. The token digest match is the sole scoping predicate (a session/role is not required pre-sign-in by design).

append_only_event.reason: Inspection is a read-only lookup by token digest. The Cycle 7A migration does not emit a workflow_events row for inspection, only for created/accepted/revoked. No durable proof of 'who viewed this invitation and when' currently exists.

failure_recovery_states: invalid token shape -> 'This invitation link is incomplete or invalid.' (client-side validInvitationToken gate, no RPC call made)
environment-unavailable -> configuration.reason surfaced, RPC not attempted
RPC error -> generic retry failure message, no partial state shown
invitation_state = expired | revoked | accepted -> distinct calm copy per state, no acceptance form rendered
contracts.persona_projection: Shows only the fields the invitation itself carries (inviter name, org name, role, location names, purpose, expiry, state); never exposes other pending invitations, roster, or case data.

contracts.capability: Invited staff member accepts an invitation, creating or updating their organization membership and location grants

frontend.note: acceptInvitation is a deliberate POST Server Action bound to a <form>; a GET receipt view may only replay an already-accepted invitation, never perform the first acceptance.

contracts.action_status: pending_acceptance -> accepted (idempotent: a same-user replay RPC call returns the same receipt with replayed=true and no duplicate rows)

durable_tables.cardinality: one row created or updated per accepted invitation; unique (organization_id, user_id) where status='active'
one row per (member, granted location); composite primary key with revoked_at upsert on replay
accepted_at/accepted_by_user_id/accepted_organization_member_id set exactly once per invitation

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: SECURITY DEFINER RPC requires auth.uid() IS NOT NULL and a verified (email_confirmed_at) email matching invitation.invited_email; the subsequent organization_member_locations select is scoped by passage_private.can_view_member_location.
failure_recovery_states: email-mismatch (42501) when the verified session email differs from the invitation's invited_email
claimed-other (23505/42501) when a matching membership is already bound to another account or needs administrator review
unavailable (22023) for invalid, expired, revoked, or already-claimed-by-a-different-user tokens
verification failure -> access remains closed if the replay RPC does not return the same organization_member_id/organization_id/member_role as the first call
previously-fixed 42702 ON CONFLICT ambiguity (migration 20260716130000) that had rolled back every first-time acceptance before the corrective migration
contracts.persona_projection: Only the accepting authenticated user's own resulting membership/receipt is returned; no other organization member, family, or vendor persona can observe an in-flight acceptance.

contracts.capability: Staff lands on /staff after sign-in or acceptance, gated by a durable, RLS-backed authority check

frontend.note: OperationalBoundary resolves the viewer server-side on every request (dynamic='force-dynamic', revalidate=0) and fails closed to 'Workspace access remains closed' rather than rendering staff content when authority is unavailable. IMPORTANT: the content rendered once the gate opens is still lib/sandbox/repository fixture data, not durable Supabase-backed assigned work -- see cycle7b.staff.assigned_work below for that gap.

contracts.action_status: auth-gate resolved per request: environment-unavailable | signed-out | membership-required | membership-selection-required | authority-unavailable | ok

durable_tables.cardinality: exactly one active row expected per (organization,user); more than one triggers membership-selection-required fail-closed
zero or more active (revoked_at is null) grants per member
read-only join to confirm status='active'

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: organization_member_locations select is scoped by passage_private.can_view_member_location(organization_member_id, organization_location_id): either the row's own member (auth.uid()) or a manager with location authority.

append_only_event.reason: Landing is a read-authorized page render, not a state mutation; no workflow_events row is emitted for a successful (or denied) landing view. See cycle7b.command_audit for the queued gap this creates.

failure_recovery_states: environment-unavailable -> 'Workspace access remains closed', no Supabase client available
signed-out -> redirect to /login with a return path
membership-required -> fail closed, no membership row
membership-selection-required -> fail closed pending disambiguation (more than one active membership)
authority-unavailable -> fail closed (unrecognized role or zero active location grants)
contracts.persona_projection: Only the signed-in user's own membership and location grants are read; no roster of other staff or organizations is exposed on this route.

contracts.capability: A staff-role member is denied access to /director (and symmetrically, an owner/director-role member is denied /staff)

frontend.note: A signed-in staff-role member hitting /director is fail-closed by canOpenOperationalPath before any director-only content renders; post-deploy browser proof recorded '/director' returning 'Workspace access remains closed' for the wrong role.

contracts.server_command: lib/auth/authorization.ts#canOpenOperationalPath(role, pathname), evaluated inside components/auth/OperationalBoundary.tsx during server render

durable_tables.cardinality: role column (owner|director|staff) read once per request to drive the denial decision

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: canOpenOperationalPath resolves the authenticated membership server-side before rendering; durable /director reads then pass through organization/location RLS on workflows, tasks, team membership, invitations, and workflow events.

append_only_event.reason: Denied navigation does not change product state, so it does not create a persona-facing command event. The boundary returns no protected rows.

failure_recovery_states: staff role at /director -> fail closed, no case/workload data fetched
owner/director role at /staff -> fail closed (symmetric)
the same environment-unavailable/signed-out/membership fail-closed states as cycle7a.staff.landing apply before the role check even runs
contracts.persona_projection: A denied viewer sees only a generic closed-access message; no case, workload, or roster data leaks across the role boundary.

contracts.capability: Staff's /staff landing shows durable, RLS-scoped assigned tasks/workflows instead of client-side sandbox fixtures

frontend.note: The verified /staff route derives the viewer from the hosted session and renders only RLS-returned durable tasks/workflows. It imports no sandbox repository, identity switcher, or local reset command.

contracts.action_status: durable assigned or in_progress tasks are re-read on every request; empty authority and empty assignment are distinct fail-closed states
contracts.server_command: RLS-scoped server SELECT over public.tasks and public.workflows through loadHostedOperations

durable_tables.cardinality: one current assigned_organization_member_id and version per durable task
one assignment/reassignment/start event per validated command idempotency key

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: passage_private.can_view_task/workflow permits active staff only when the exact task is assigned to their active membership and the workflow location grant is active; manager projections use managed organization/location authority

append_only_event.idempotency_key: caller-supplied stable UUID, unique within organization

failure_recovery_states: reload/reconnect re-reads durable assignment truth
cross-organization, cross-location, unassigned, reassigned, and revoked reads return no task rows
contracts.persona_projection: Staff sees only tasks assigned to their own active membership; director sees the same durable task and event identifiers; family and vendors receive no internal work projection.

contracts.capability: Director reassigns a commitment/task to another staff member with durable RLS authority and an audit trail, replacing the in-memory sandbox reassignment dispatch

frontend.note: Director Today submits the durable task id, expected version, location-authorized assignee, reason, and stable request UUID through a Server Action. Success is shown only from the RPC receipt and a route refresh.

contracts.action_status: assigned or in_progress -> same state with a new assigned member, incremented version, required reason, and durable receipt
contracts.server_command: public.assign_task_idempotent(p_task_id uuid, p_expected_version integer, p_assignee_member_id uuid, p_reason text, p_request_id uuid)

durable_tables.cardinality: one current assignee and monotonically increasing version per task
one task.reassigned row per organization/request UUID

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: RPC requires active owner/director authority on the workflow organization and location and locks an active staff assignee with an active grant on that same location

append_only_event.idempotency_key: caller-supplied stable request UUID, unique within organization

failure_recovery_states: stale expected version fails with no partial write and offers a reload
wrong organization/location/inactive assignee and same-key conflicting payload fail closed
same-key replay returns the original receipt without a duplicate event
contracts.persona_projection: Director/owner sees and changes only managed-location work; the former staff projection loses the task on re-read and the new assignee sees the same durable task identifier.

contracts.capability: Director/owner revokes a pending invitation through a director-facing UI with a visible pending-invitation list and audit surfacing

frontend.note: Team lists only RLS-returned invitations. Pending rows expose a reason-required revoke action; accepted rows direct the director to membership access instead.

contracts.action_status: pending_acceptance -> revoked; replay returns the same durable receipt and no access is granted

durable_tables.cardinality: already implemented (Cycle 7A) -- revoked_at/revoked_by_user_id/revocation_reason columns with a shape check constraint

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: already implemented: passage_private.can_manage_invitation(id) gate inside the RPC; no direct table UPDATE grant exists for authenticated (writes only via the SECURITY DEFINER function)
failure_recovery_states: accepted invitations reject revocation and Team points to membership access
same-state replay returns the existing receipt without a duplicate event
wrong organization/location/role fails closed and the form reports that nothing changed
contracts.persona_projection: Director/owner sees pending invitations only within managed organization/location authority; staff, family, and vendors receive no invitation roster.

contracts.capability: Director-facing, append-only command/audit trail (who did what, when, to what, with what proof) per the persona-action-architecture's universal action contract

frontend.note: Activity is a read-only semantic event list showing actor, role, action/target, location, previous/next state, reason, server time, proof id, and visibility from the RLS-scoped event spine.

contracts.action_status: read-only, server-rendered projection of durable workflow_events ordered by occurred_at
contracts.server_command: RLS-scoped server SELECT against public.workflow_events through loadHostedOperations

durable_tables.cardinality: one append-only row per accepted idempotent invitation/assignment/start/revocation command

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: passage_private.can_view_workflow_event requires active organization management and every location carried by the event metadata to be managed; partial-location access cannot read a multi-location revocation event

append_only_event.reason: Activity is a read surface and introduces no mutation; each command contract defines its own event type and idempotency key.

failure_recovery_states: wrong organization/location reads return no events
authority verification failure renders no activity and offers retry
event update/delete is rejected by the append-only trigger
contracts.persona_projection: Owner/director sees only managed organization/location events; staff, family, and vendors receive no operator audit projection.

contracts.capability: Director views durable organization/location-scoped workload and ownership instead of the sandbox repository

frontend.note: Director Today renders durable RLS-scoped workflows/tasks with accountable owner, assignee, waiting party, due state, status, and the bounded assignment action. It imports no sandbox repository.

contracts.action_status: durable workload rows grouped by case/workflow with accountable owner, assignee, waiting party, due state, status, and next action
contracts.server_command: RLS-scoped server SELECT over public.workflows and public.tasks through loadHostedOperations

durable_tables.cardinality: one durable workflow per case at one organization/location
durable commitments reference the same workflow, organization, and assigned member used by staff

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: passage_private.can_view_workflow/task requires active owner/director membership plus an active grant on the workflow location; any location filtering is presentation only

append_only_event.reason: This contract is a read-only workload projection; assignment mutations are tracked separately.

failure_recovery_states: wrong-organization and wrong-location reads return no rows without revealing identifiers
unavailable or revoked membership renders a calm closed-access recovery with no workload flash
contracts.persona_projection: A director sees only workloads at locations they manage; staff, family, and vendors never receive the director workload projection.

contracts.capability: Director assigns an unowned durable commitment to an active location-authorized staff member

frontend.note: Unassigned Director Today rows expose the same durable CommandForms assignment action used for reassignment, with a stable request UUID and expected task version.

contracts.action_status: unassigned -> assigned with version increment, current/new owner, waiting party, reason, event proof, and next status returned
contracts.server_command: public.assign_task_idempotent(p_task_id uuid, p_expected_version integer, p_assignee_member_id uuid, p_reason text, p_request_id uuid)

durable_tables.cardinality: one current assigned_organization_member_id and version per task
one task.assigned event per organization/request UUID

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: Active owner/director may assign only a managed-location task to an active staff member with an active grant on that exact location

append_only_event.idempotency_key: caller-supplied stable request UUID, unique within organization

failure_recovery_states: same-key replay returns the durable assignment without a second event
stale version and inactive, wrong-location, wrong-organization, null, or conflicting assignee fail without partial writes
contracts.persona_projection: Directors see the selected owner; the assigned staff member sees the same durable task identifier in My work.

contracts.capability: Assigned staff member starts one durable commitment from My work

frontend.note: Each assigned My work card exposes only the bounded Start work transition. Proof attachment, blocker, completion, and output-review claims remain hidden and queued.

contracts.action_status: assigned -> in_progress with incremented version, server actor/time, event proof, and durable route refresh
contracts.server_command: public.start_task_idempotent(p_task_id uuid, p_expected_version integer, p_request_id uuid)

durable_tables.cardinality: one current durable status and version per assigned task
one task.started event per organization/request UUID

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: Caller must be the task's current active assigned staff member with an active grant on the workflow location; task and member rows are locked before mutation

append_only_event.idempotency_key: caller-supplied stable request UUID, unique within organization

failure_recovery_states: unassigned, reassigned, revoked, wrong-location, stale-version, and invalid-state attempts fail without changing the task
same-key replay by the current assignee returns the existing durable state/event; a former assignee cannot replay after reassignment
contracts.persona_projection: Staff sees minimum assigned-case context, human action, proof destination, and next state; director sees the same task/event; family receives no internal work detail.

contracts.capability: Director revokes an active staff membership and its location authority with durable audit proof

frontend.note: Team exposes End team access only when the member has zero active commitments; otherwise it blocks the command and names the exact reassignment count.

contracts.action_status: active membership -> revoked with all current location grants ended in the same transaction and one durable receipt

durable_tables.cardinality: one revoked_at/revoked_by/reason terminal state for the member
every current grant receives one revoked_at/revoked_by/reason state in the same transaction
one organization_membership.revoked event per organization/request UUID

contracts.rls_authority_predicate: Active owner/director must manage the organization and every currently granted location; only staff may be revoked and any active task assignment blocks the command

append_only_event.idempotency_key: caller-supplied stable request UUID, unique within organization

failure_recovery_states: same-key replay returns the existing revocation without a duplicate event
active assignments fail with no membership or grant change and Team requires reassignment first
revoked user's next request/reload loses staff and director-route access and sees the distinct access-ended state
contracts.persona_projection: Director sees immutable revoked status and historical locations; revoked staff sees closed access; family and vendors see no membership detail.
